title: OCC Sandler Coaching Prompt Library
description: Coaching prompts mapped to call scoring schema - delivered same-day after calls

compartment1_bondingRapport.objective: Build trust and emotional safety so prospects tell you the truth

commonIssues.issue: Jumps straight to questions without rapport
commonIssues.detectedWhen: Call starts with qualifying questions in first 60 seconds

coachingPrompt.headline: ⚡ Build rapport BEFORE interrogating
coachingPrompt.problem: You jumped straight into qualifying questions. Prospects shut down when they feel interrogated.
coachingPrompt.impact: Without rapport, you get surface-level answers. They won't share real pain.
coachingPrompt.fix: Spend 2-3 minutes building rapport first. Show genuine curiosity about THEIR world.
sayThis: Out of curiosity, how did you end up responsible for [their area]?
Help me understand your situation...
Before we dive in, tell me a bit about your world...
notThat: ❌ So, how many reps do you have? (Too abrupt)
❌ Let me ask you some questions... (Interrogation mode)
❌ Tell me about your pain points. (Generic)
coachingPrompt.practice: On your next 3 calls, open with 'Out of curiosity...' and see how they open up.

commonIssues.detectedWhen: Rep says 'I'd love to earn your business' or 'When can I follow up?'

coachingPrompt.headline: 🎯 Equal stature: You're peers, not subordinate
coachingPrompt.problem: You used subordinate language that positions you as a vendor begging for business.
coachingPrompt.impact: When you sound needy, prospects leverage that. They sense desperation and discount you.
coachingPrompt.fix: Position as equals. You're both evaluating if this makes sense.
sayThis: Let's figure out if this makes sense for both of us
This may or may not be a fit, and that's okay
If this isn't right, are you comfortable telling me no?
notThat: ❌ I'd love to earn your business
❌ When can I follow up with you?
❌ What can I do to win this deal?
coachingPrompt.practice: Replace every 'I'd love to...' with 'Let's figure out if...' for one week.

commonIssues.detectedWhen: Rep talk time > 50% in bonding phase

coachingPrompt.headline: 🔇 Listen more than you talk
coachingPrompt.problem: You talked 65% of the time in the first 5 minutes. Prospects don't trust people who monologue.
coachingPrompt.impact: When you talk too much, you don't learn. And prospects feel sold TO, not heard.
coachingPrompt.fix: Aim for 30-40% talk time in discovery. Ask, then SHUT UP and listen.
sayThis: [Ask question] → [3-5 second silence]
Tell me more about that...
Help me understand...
notThat: ❌ [Ask question then immediately add 3 more sentences]
❌ [Fill every silence with more talking]
❌ Oh I know exactly what you mean, we have clients who...
coachingPrompt.practice: After asking a question, count to 5 silently before saying ANYTHING. Tolerate the silence.

compartment2_upFrontContract.objective: Set clear expectations and make 'no' safe BEFORE diving in

commonIssues.detectedWhen: No mention of time, agenda, or permission to say no

coachingPrompt.headline: 🚨 ALWAYS set an up-front contract
coachingPrompt.problem: You jumped straight into questions without setting expectations. This creates hidden resistance.
coachingPrompt.impact: Without a contract, prospects feel ambushed. They're thinking 'How long is this? Where's this going? Can I say no?' instead of focusing on your questions.
coachingPrompt.fix: Set an up-front contract EVERY TIME. Takes 30 seconds, saves 30 minutes of wasted calls.
coachingPrompt.template: So that we respect your time, here's what I'd like to do: [AGENDA]. We have [TIME]. If we get to the end and it's not a fit, are you comfortable telling me no so we don't create maybes? Fair enough?
sayThis: I have a few questions to see if we're even a fit. Fair enough?
If this isn't right, are you comfortable saying no?
We said 15 minutes - does that still work?
notThat: ❌ [Immediately starts asking questions]
❌ This will only take a minute... [then goes 20 minutes]
❌ So tell me about your business...
coachingPrompt.practice: Script your up-front contract word-for-word. Memorize it. Use it on EVERY call for 2 weeks.

commonIssues.detectedWhen: Contract sets time/agenda but doesn't give permission to say no

coachingPrompt.headline: ✅ Make 'no' safe and explicit
coachingPrompt.problem: You set time and agenda, but you didn't give them permission to say no. They're still guarded.
coachingPrompt.impact: If 'no' isn't safe, prospects create maybes. They ghost you instead of being honest.
coachingPrompt.fix: Explicitly say: 'If this isn't a fit, are you comfortable telling me no?'
sayThis: If we get to the end and it's not a fit, are you comfortable telling me no so we don't create maybes?
This may not be for you, and that's okay
No is a perfectly acceptable answer - I promise I won't be offended
notThat: ❌ I think you're going to love this
❌ [Assumes they'll say yes if they're interested]
❌ I'm confident we can help you
coachingPrompt.practice: End your up-front contract with 'Fair enough?' and WAIT for them to say yes.

compartment3_pain.objective: Go from surface symptoms → deep business impact → personal consequences → commitment

commonIssues.issue: Spills candy in lobby (pitches before pain)
commonIssues.detectedWhen: Mentions product features before completing pain funnel

coachingPrompt.headline: 🍬 DON'T SPILL YOUR CANDY IN THE LOBBY
coachingPrompt.problem: You jumped to features in minute 4. They asked 'How does it work?' and you answered. You gave away your expertise too early.
coachingPrompt.impact: When you pitch before understanding deep pain, you lose leverage. They got what they wanted (information) without committing to anything.
coachingPrompt.fix: REVERSE the question. Answer with a question. Don't spill your candy until you're qualified.
sayThis: Good question - what specifically are you trying to solve that's driving that question?
Before I answer that, can we talk about what's not working today?
I could show you how it works, but if it doesn't solve your specific problem, that's a waste of time. What's going on?
notThat: ❌ Oh great question! Here's how it works... [launches into demo]
❌ We have an AI that scores calls and... [feature dump]
❌ Let me show you... [screen share]
coachingPrompt.practice: Next 5 times someone asks 'How does it work?' — REVERSE with 'What specifically are you trying to solve?' Count how many go deeper into pain.

commonIssues.issue: Stays at surface pain (doesn't go deep)
commonIssues.detectedWhen: Asks fewer than 4 pain questions OR doesn't get to personal impact

coachingPrompt.headline: ⬇️ Go DEEPER on pain - use the Pain Funnel
coachingPrompt.problem: You stopped at surface pain. They said 'reps aren't using the methodology' and you moved on. That's level 1 of 7.
coachingPrompt.impact: Surface pain doesn't create urgency. They'll say 'Let me think about it' because you didn't connect it to real cost or personal consequences.
coachingPrompt.fix: Use the Pain Funnel to go 6-7 levels deep. Surface → Impact → Personal → Commitment.
painFunnelLevels: Level 1 (Surface): 'Tell me more about that.' 'Can you give me an example?'
Level 2 (Duration): 'How long has that been happening?'
Level 3 (Impact): 'What does that cost the business?' 'What does that do to your pipeline?'
Level 4 (Personal): 'How does that affect YOU personally?' 'How does that make you feel?'
Level 5 (Consequences): 'What happens if nothing changes?' 'What does that mean for your team?'
Level 6 (Gave up?): 'Have you given up trying to fix this?'
Level 7 (Why now?): 'Why fix it now versus six months from now?'
sayThis: Tell me more about that → How long has that been an issue? → What's that costing you? → How does that affect YOU personally? → What happens if nothing changes?
[After they share surface pain] → Can you give me an example? → What does that do to your numbers? → How does that land on you?
Why now versus waiting another quarter?
notThat: ❌ [Hears surface pain] → Oh we can help with that! [pitches]
❌ [Asks one pain question] → Great, so let me show you...
❌ [Doesn't ask about personal impact]
coachingPrompt.practice: On your next call, ask 7 pain questions in a row. See how deep you can go before they say 'That's exactly why I need to fix this NOW.'

commonIssues.detectedWhen: Rapid-fire questions without pauses or acknowledgment

coachingPrompt.headline: 🧭 Explore pain, don't interrogate
coachingPrompt.problem: You asked 6 pain questions in 90 seconds without pausing. It felt like an interrogation.
coachingPrompt.impact: When you rapid-fire questions, prospects shut down. They give shorter answers and guard the truth.
coachingPrompt.fix: Ask a question → PAUSE 3-5 seconds → Acknowledge what they said → Ask next question.
sayThis: [Ask question] → [Wait 3-5 seconds] → [Let them finish] → 'That sounds frustrating' → [Next question]
Tell me more about that... [silence]
Help me understand how that affects you... [silence]
notThat: ❌ How long has that been happening? What's it costing? How does that affect you? [machine gun]
❌ [Asks question, prospect starts answering, rep interrupts with next question]
❌ [Zero pauses, zero acknowledgment]
coachingPrompt.practice: After EVERY pain question, count to 5 silently. Let them think. Let them expand. Silence is your friend.

commonIssues.detectedWhen: Opportunity to pull back but rep keeps pushing forward

coachingPrompt.headline: ↔️ Advanced: Use the Pendulum technique
coachingPrompt.problem: You got great pain, but you didn't pull back. They said it's costing 3-4 deals a quarter, and you immediately moved to budget.
coachingPrompt.impact: When you always push forward, you create resistance. Pull back to create psychological desire.
coachingPrompt.fix: After uncovering pain, PULL BACK with negative reverse. Let THEM chase YOU.
sayThis: Honestly, if it's only 3-4 deals a quarter, this might not be worth changing.
Maybe sticking with your current approach is the right move.
I'm not sure we're the right fit here - this seems pretty minor.
If the team is okay with the current results, you probably don't need this.
notThat: ❌ [Hears pain] → Great! Let me show you how we solve that! [pushes]
❌ [Immediately moves to next compartment without pulling back]
❌ So let's talk about budget... [too eager]
coachingPrompt.psychology: When you pull back, the prospect's brain says 'Wait, I DO have a problem!' and they lean in. This is the Pendulum: pull back before pushing forward.
coachingPrompt.practice: Next time you uncover strong pain (7+ out of 10), pull back with 'Maybe this isn't worth fixing.' Watch them INSIST it is.

compartment4_budget.objective: Normalize money conversation. Qualify ability AND willingness to invest.

commonIssues.detectedWhen: Moves to decision or fulfillment without discussing budget

coachingPrompt.headline: 💰 ALWAYS talk about money
coachingPrompt.problem: You skipped budget entirely. You got to decision without knowing if they have $500/month.
coachingPrompt.impact: You'll waste weeks on deals that can't afford you. Budget is a qualifier, not a closer.
coachingPrompt.fix: Normalize money. Make it conversational. Ask BEFORE you invest time in fulfillment.
sayThis: Can we talk a bit about budget so we both know whether this even makes sense to keep going?
How do you usually decide what's worth investing in for this kind of problem?
What were you thinking of investing to solve this?
Just so I know where we are - do you have budget allocated or would this need approval?
notThat: ❌ [Skips budget, hopes they'll figure it out later]
❌ We'll talk pricing after the demo
❌ [Waits for them to ask 'How much?']
coachingPrompt.practice: On your next 3 calls, say this exact phrase: 'Can we talk about budget so we both know if this makes sense?' See how they respond.

commonIssues.issue: Gives price before pain is established
commonIssues.detectedWhen: Prospect asks 'How much?' early and rep answers without reversing

coachingPrompt.headline: 🔄 REVERSE pricing questions if asked too early
coachingPrompt.problem: They asked 'What does this cost?' in minute 3 and you answered. You spilled your candy.
coachingPrompt.impact: When you give price before pain, they anchor on cost without understanding value. You lose leverage.
coachingPrompt.fix: REVERSE the pricing question. Tie it back to pain.
sayThis: Fair question - it varies quite a bit depending on what you need. Can we talk about what you're trying to solve first so I can give you a meaningful range?
Good question. Before I answer that - what were you thinking of investing to solve [their pain]?
I could throw a number at you, but if it doesn't solve your problem, price doesn't matter. What's going on?
notThat: ❌ It's $50 per rep per month [too early]
❌ I'll send you pricing after this call [dodging]
❌ [Gives price before understanding pain/budget]
coachingPrompt.practice: Next time someone asks price early, reverse with: 'What were YOU thinking of investing to solve this?' and see what they say.

commonIssues.detectedWhen: Prospect says 'We'll find budget if it makes sense' and rep moves on

coachingPrompt.headline: 🎯 Test if budget is REAL
coachingPrompt.problem: They said 'We'll find budget if it makes sense' and you accepted that. That's not a real budget.
coachingPrompt.impact: Vague budget = deal dies in procurement. You need to know if they CAN and WILL invest.
coachingPrompt.fix: Test the budget. Use negative reverse if it's vague.
sayThis: 'We'll find budget' - I've heard that before and it usually means there's no budget. Should we reconnect when budget is actually allocated?
So there's no budget allocated yet - is that right? Who would need to approve this and what's that process?
If $500/month isn't in the budget, let's not waste your time with a demo.
notThat: ❌ Great, we'll find budget! [too optimistic]
❌ [Accepts vague answer and moves forward]
❌ I'm sure you can get it approved [assumes]
coachingPrompt.practice: When you hear vague budget answers, use negative reverse: 'Sounds like there's no budget - should we pause this?' Forces them to reveal truth.

compartment5_decision.objective: Understand WHO decides, HOW they decide, WHEN. Flush roadblocks with 'What then?'

commonIssues.detectedWhen: Doesn't ask 'Who else is involved?'

coachingPrompt.headline: 👥 NEVER assume they're the decision maker
coachingPrompt.problem: You moved to fulfillment without asking who else is involved. Then at the end they said 'I need to run this by my VP.'
coachingPrompt.impact: Surprise decision makers kill deals. You pitched to the wrong person and now you're stuck.
coachingPrompt.fix: ALWAYS map the decision committee. Assume they're NOT the sole decision maker.
sayThis: Besides you, who else will be involved in deciding whether we move forward?
Walk me through who's in the room when this gets decided.
Who else has to sign off on this?
If you say yes, what happens next? Who else weighs in?
notThat: ❌ [Assumes they can decide alone]
❌ So should we move forward? [without understanding decision process]
❌ Great, I'll send over a contract [then finds out CFO has to approve]
coachingPrompt.practice: On EVERY call, ask 'Besides you, who else is involved?' Even if they're the CEO. See what you uncover.

commonIssues.detectedWhen: Knows who decides but not HOW they decide

coachingPrompt.headline: 🗺️ Map the PROCESS, not just the people
coachingPrompt.problem: You asked who's involved, but you didn't understand the PROCESS. How does this actually get decided?
coachingPrompt.impact: Without understanding the process, you can't navigate it. The deal stalls and you don't know why.
coachingPrompt.fix: Ask 'Walk me through how decisions like this usually get made here.'
sayThis: Walk me through how decisions like this usually get made at your company.
What does a 'yes' actually look like procedurally?
What has to happen between now and a signed contract?
What's your normal process for evaluating new vendors?
notThat: ❌ [Knows who decides but not the steps]
❌ So you'll take it to your team? [vague]
❌ [Doesn't understand approval process]
coachingPrompt.practice: After you map WHO is involved, ask 'Walk me through how this usually gets decided.' Then use 'What then?' to go deeper.

commonIssues.issue: Doesn't use 'What then?' to flush roadblocks
commonIssues.detectedWhen: Accepts decision process at face value without going deeper

coachingPrompt.headline: 🔍 Advanced: Flush roadblocks with 'What then?'
coachingPrompt.problem: They said 'I present to my VP, then we decide.' You accepted that. You didn't ask 'What then?'
coachingPrompt.impact: Hidden roadblocks kill deals. 'What then?' questions reveal obstacles BEFORE they surprise you.
coachingPrompt.fix: After each step in the decision process, ask 'What then?' or 'And then what happens?' Go 3-4 levels deep.
sayThis: So you present to your VP... what then?
And then what happens?
What has to happen after that?
What could derail this between now and signing?
Who might push back and why?
notThat: ❌ [Accepts 'I'll take it to my team' without drilling down]
❌ [Doesn't uncover hidden objections]
❌ Sounds good! [without understanding what could go wrong]
coachingPrompt.example:
You: 'So you present to your VP... what then?'
Them: 'She'll want to see ROI data.'
You: 'And then what happens?'
Them: 'Then it goes to finance for approval.'
You: 'What then?'
Them: 'Well, finance will want to see if we have budget this quarter... which we might not.'

→ You just found the real roadblock.
coachingPrompt.practice: On your next call, after they describe the decision process, ask 'What then?' 4 times in a row. Count how many roadblocks you uncover.

commonIssues.detectedWhen: Rep tries to force their process onto prospect's buying system

coachingPrompt.headline: 🔧 Prospect's system ALWAYS wins
coachingPrompt.problem: They said 'We need to do a 30-day trial first' and you pushed back with 'We don't usually do trials.' Their system always wins.
coachingPrompt.impact: When you fight their process, you lose. Accept that their buying system will win and work within it.
coachingPrompt.fix: Ask 'Help me understand how this typically works at your company' and ADAPT to their system.
sayThis: Help me understand how this typically works at your company.
What's your normal process for evaluating new vendors?
Walk me through what happens on your side.
[After they explain] → Okay, so we need to work within that. What's the best way to do this given your process?
notThat: ❌ We don't usually do trials [fighting their system]
❌ Our process is... [imposing your process]
❌ Can we skip the committee and just decide? [unrealistic]
coachingPrompt.practice: When they describe their buying process, say 'Help me understand how that works' instead of explaining YOUR process.

compartment6_fulfillment.objective: Present solution ONLY after qualifying. Tie features to their specific pains.

commonIssues.detectedWhen: Jumps to fulfillment before pain/budget/decision are covered

coachingPrompt.headline: ⛔ DON'T present until you're QUALIFIED
coachingPrompt.problem: You started presenting in minute 6. Pain, budget, and decision weren't covered. You spilled your candy.
coachingPrompt.impact: When you present too early, you have no leverage. They got your expertise for free and now they'll shop you.
coachingPrompt.fix: Seal compartments 1-5 BEFORE fulfillment. No exceptions.
checklist: ✅ Bonding & Rapport: Do they trust you?
✅ Pain: Did you go 6-7 levels deep?
✅ Budget: Do you know if they can afford it?
✅ Decision: Do you know who decides and how?
sayThis: [Before presenting] → 'Based on what you've shared - [recap their pain] - here's how we'd solve that...'
[If they ask for demo before qualifying] → 'Happy to show you, but can we talk about what you're solving first so I know what to focus on?'
notThat: ❌ Let me show you how it works! [without qualifying]
❌ [Starts demo in minute 5]
❌ [Pitches before understanding pain/budget/decision]
coachingPrompt.practice: Next 5 calls: don't present ANYTHING until compartments 1-5 are sealed. Force yourself to qualify first.

commonIssues.issue: Generic pitch (doesn't tie to their specific pains)
commonIssues.detectedWhen: Uses generic benefits instead of referencing their stated pains

coachingPrompt.headline: 🎯 Map features to THEIR pains, not generic benefits
coachingPrompt.problem: You said 'We score calls and provide coaching.' That's generic. You didn't reference the pains they told you.
coachingPrompt.impact: Generic pitches sound like spam. Prospects tune out because you're not speaking to THEIR problem.
coachingPrompt.fix: Reference the EXACT pains they mentioned. Use THEIR words, not yours.
coachingPrompt.template: You mentioned [THEIR PAIN] - here's how we solve that: [FEATURE TIED TO THAT SPECIFIC PAIN]. Does that address what you were talking about?
sayThis: You mentioned reps revert to old habits after training - we solve that by scoring EVERY call so you catch drift before deals stall.
You said you can't prove ROI on training - our dashboard shows methodology adherence vs close rates, so you can prove what's working.
Remember you said it's costing 3-4 deals a quarter? This addresses that by coaching same-day instead of weeks later.
notThat: ❌ We have AI that scores calls [generic]
❌ Our platform provides coaching [not tied to their pain]
❌ We integrate with Gong and Salesforce [features without context]
coachingPrompt.practice: Before presenting ANY feature, say 'You mentioned [their pain]...' to tie it back to what they told you.

commonIssues.detectedWhen: Talks for 5+ minutes straight without asking 'How does this land?'

coachingPrompt.headline: 💬 Keep fulfillment CONVERSATIONAL
coachingPrompt.problem: You talked for 7 minutes straight in fulfillment. That's a monologue, not a conversation.
coachingPrompt.impact: When you monologue, you lose them. They zone out. You don't know if it's landing.
coachingPrompt.fix: Present for 2-3 minutes MAX, then check in. Make it a dialogue.
sayThis: [Present for 2-3 minutes] → 'How does this land compared to what you were hoping to see?'
Does this address what you mentioned earlier about [pain]?
What questions do you have so far?
I'm sensing some hesitation - what's not sitting right?
notThat: ❌ [Talks for 7+ minutes without pausing]
❌ [Screen shares and walks through every feature]
❌ [Doesn't check if it's landing]
coachingPrompt.practice: Set a timer. Present for 2 minutes, then ask 'How does this land?' Repeat. Never monologue for more than 2 minutes.

compartment7_postSell.objective: Prevent buyer's remorse. Flush objections BEFORE they happen.

commonIssues.detectedWhen: Prospect agrees to move forward and rep immediately ends call

coachingPrompt.headline: 🛡️ ALWAYS do post-sell - prevent buyer's remorse
coachingPrompt.problem: They said yes and you ended the call. You didn't prevent buyer's remorse or flush objections.
coachingPrompt.impact: Deals die AFTER the yes. They ghost you because they got cold feet and you didn't address it.
coachingPrompt.fix: After they agree, do post-sell. Recap, flush objections, set success criteria.
sayThis: Great! Just to recap, we've agreed on [X, Y, Z]. Does that still feel right?
Who might push back on this internally? How will you handle that?
What's the first sign this isn't going as planned?
What would make you regret this decision in 90 days?
notThat: ❌ Great! I'll send over the contract. Talk soon! [ends call]
❌ [Assumes smooth sailing after yes]
❌ [Doesn't address potential objections]
coachingPrompt.practice: After EVERY yes, ask 'Who might push back internally?' See how many hidden objections you uncover.

commonIssues.detectedWhen: Doesn't ask who might object or how they'll handle it

coachingPrompt.headline: 🚧 Flush internal roadblocks NOW
coachingPrompt.problem: They said yes, but you didn't ask who might push back. Deals die from internal objections you didn't see coming.
coachingPrompt.impact: CFO, VP, or teammate will object later. If you don't prepare the champion now, they'll crumble.
coachingPrompt.fix: Ask 'Who might push back?' and 'How will you handle that?' before they leave the call.
sayThis: Who might push back on this internally?
How do you think finance will react when they see this?
What will you say when your VP asks why now?
Who's going to be skeptical and why?
What objections do you expect and how will you handle them?
notThat: ❌ [Assumes no one will object]
❌ I'm sure your team will love this [overly optimistic]
❌ [Doesn't prepare champion for objections]
coachingPrompt.practice: After they agree, ask 'Who might push back and why?' Then ask 'What will you say to them?' Coach them through it.

commonIssues.issue: Uses negative reverse too early (before stall)
commonIssues.detectedWhen: Uses 'Should we take this off the table?' during active discussion

coachingPrompt.headline: ⏰ Timing: Negative reverse ONLY at stall points
coachingPrompt.problem: You used negative reverse while they were still engaged. 'Should we take this off the table?' before there was a stall.
coachingPrompt.impact: Negative reverse at the wrong time backfires. They say 'Yeah, let's pause' when they were actually moving forward.
coachingPrompt.fix: ONLY use negative reverse when the deal is STALLING. Not before.
sayThis: [ONLY when stalled] → 'Should we just take this off the table for now?'
[ONLY when they go dark] → 'Honestly, it feels like this may not be the right time. Should we reconnect when it's more urgent?'
[ONLY when timeline slips] → 'Maybe we should pause this until you have more clarity.'
notThat: ❌ [Uses negative reverse during active discussion]
❌ [Uses it as a closing technique]
❌ [Uses it when they're still moving forward]
coachingPrompt.practice: Save negative reverse for when deals STALL. If they're moving forward, don't use it.

tonalityCoaching.objective: Sound calm, curious, and detached - not desperate or interrogating

commonIssues.detectedWhen: Voice spikes with excitement when prospect shows interest

coachingPrompt.headline: 😌 Stay calm - be okay with NO
coachingPrompt.problem: Your voice spiked when they said 'This sounds interesting.' You sounded excited/desperate.
coachingPrompt.impact: Prospects sense desperation and leverage it. They discount you or ghost you.
coachingPrompt.fix: Calm detachment. You're evaluating if THEY'RE a fit, not begging for business.
sayThis: [Calm, even tone] → 'Let's figure out if this makes sense for both of us.'
[Matter-of-fact] → 'This may or may not be a fit, and that's okay.'
[Genuinely detached] → 'I'm not sure we're the right fit here.'
notThat: ❌ [Excited spike] → 'Oh that's GREAT! We can definitely help!'
❌ [Eager tone] → 'I'd LOVE to work with you!'
❌ [Desperate] → 'When can we get started?!'
coachingPrompt.practice: Record your next 3 calls. Listen for voice spikes. Practice calm, even delivery.

commonIssues.detectedWhen: Questions sound like cross-examination instead of exploration

coachingPrompt.headline: 🧭 Be CURIOUS, not interrogating
coachingPrompt.problem: Your questions sounded like an interrogation. 'How many reps? What's your close rate? How much training?' - rapid fire, no warmth.
coachingPrompt.impact: Interrogation = resistance. Curiosity = openness. Your tone determines what they share.
coachingPrompt.fix: Soften your questions. Sound genuinely curious about their world.
sayThis: [Curious] → 'Out of curiosity, how did you end up in this role?'
[Exploratory] → 'Help me understand what's going on...'
[Warm] → 'Tell me more about that...' [with genuine interest]
notThat: ❌ [Interrogating] → 'How many reps do you have?'
❌ [Transactional] → 'What's your budget?'
❌ [Robotic] → 'Tell me about your pain points.'
coachingPrompt.practice: Start every question with 'Out of curiosity...' or 'Help me understand...' for one week. Notice how responses change.

advancedTechniques.objective: Master social layer patterns for maximum effectiveness

techniques.when: After uncovering strong pain (7+ out of 10)
techniques.how: Use negative reverse to pull back. Let THEM chase YOU.
examples: Honestly, if it's only costing a few deals a quarter, this might not be worth changing.
Maybe sticking with your current approach is actually the best move.
I'm not sure we're the right fit - this seems pretty minor.
techniques.psychology: When you pull back, their brain says 'Wait, I DO have a problem!' Pendulum creates psychological desire.
techniques.practice: Next time you uncover 8/10 pain, pull back with 'Maybe this isn't worth fixing.' Count how often they INSIST it is.

techniques.when: During Decision compartment to uncover hidden obstacles
techniques.how: After they describe each step in decision process, ask 'What then?' Go 3-4 levels deep.
examples: So you present to your VP... what then?
What has to happen after that?
techniques.psychology: 'What then?' forces them to think through the REAL process. Hidden roadblocks surface.
techniques.practice: On your next call, ask 'What then?' 4 times in a row during decision mapping. See what you uncover.

techniques.when: When prospect describes their buying process
techniques.how: Accept their system. Adapt to it. Don't fight it.
examples: Help me understand how this typically works at your company.
What's your normal process for evaluating vendors?
Walk me through what happens on your side.
techniques.psychology: Their buying system will always win. Fighting it creates resistance. Adapting builds trust.
techniques.practice: When they describe their process, say 'Help me understand how that works' instead of explaining YOUR process.

techniques.technique: Negative Reverse at Stall Points ONLY
techniques.when: Deal is stalling, timeline slips, or prospect goes dark
techniques.how: Pull the deal OFF the table. Force them to reveal truth.
examples: Should we just take this off the table for now?
Honestly, it feels like this may not be the right time.
Maybe we should pause this until you have more clarity.
techniques.psychology: When you pull back at a stall, they either confirm it's dead (saving you time) or re-engage (revealing real objection).
techniques.practice: Next time a deal goes dark for 2+ weeks, send: 'Should we take this off the table?' See what happens.

coachingDeliveryGuidelines.timing: Deliver coaching same-day after call (while fresh)
coachingDeliveryGuidelines.tone: Direct but supportive. Focus on IMPROVEMENT, not criticism.
coachingDeliveryGuidelines.format: Headline → Problem → Impact → Fix → Say This/Not That → Practice

prioritization.highPriority: Critical mistakes that kill deals (spilling candy, skipping compartments)

coachingDeliveryGuidelines.practiceAssignments: Every coaching prompt includes a specific practice assignment for next 3-5 calls
coachingDeliveryGuidelines.videoReferences: Each prompt links to specific Sandler training module timestamp
coachingDeliveryGuidelines.progressTracking: Track improvement over time - are they fixing the issues or repeating them?
